title: Quiz: Quantum Gravity and Information
description: Test your understanding of the black hole information paradox, Page curves, and the island formula

questions.question: What is the fundamental conflict at the heart of the black hole information paradox?

options.text: Black holes cannot form according to quantum mechanics
Hawking radiation appears thermal, seemingly destroying information, which violates unitarity

questions.explanation: The information paradox arises because Hawking radiation appears to be perfectly thermal, carrying no information about what fell into the black hole. If this is true, then pure states evolve into mixed states during black hole evaporation, violating the unitarity of quantum mechanics.

questions.question: What is the Page time in black hole evaporation?

options.text: The time when the black hole completely evaporates
The time when the black hole first forms
The time when the radiation entropy peaks and equals the remaining black hole entropy
The scrambling time of the black hole

questions.explanation: The Page time is when the entanglement entropy of Hawking radiation peaks. At this point, about half the black hole's initial entropy has been radiated. Before the Page time, entropy increases; after it, entropy should decrease if evolution is unitary. This is when the Page curve transitions.

questions.question: What does the island formula compute?

options.text: The fine-grained entanglement entropy of radiation by extremizing over island regions
The mass of the black hole

questions.explanation: The island formula computes the fine-grained entropy of radiation as: S(R) = min ext [A(boundary of island)/(4G) + S_bulk(R union island)]. By including 'islands' in the black hole interior, this formula reproduces the Page curve and resolves the information paradox.

questions.question: In the Hayden-Preskill protocol, after the Page time, how many additional Hawking quanta does Bob need to recover Alice's diary?

options.text: Only O(k + 1) qubits, where k is the diary size
Zero - the information is already available

questions.explanation: Remarkably, after the Page time and scrambling time, Bob needs only O(k + O(1)) additional Hawking quanta to recover k qubits of information, provided he already has access to the early radiation. This shows that old black holes rapidly 'release' information.

questions.question: The firewall paradox arises from the conflict between the monogamy of entanglement and the requirements for both a smooth horizon and a unitary Page curve.
questions.explanation: True. The AMPS firewall paradox points out that late radiation (A) cannot be maximally entangled with both early radiation (B) and interior modes (C) simultaneously due to monogamy of entanglement. But the Page curve requires A+C entangled with B, while a smooth horizon requires A entangled with C.

questions.question: A quantum extremal surface (QES) extremizes which quantity?
questions.explanation: A quantum extremal surface extremizes the generalized entropy S_gen = A/(4G) + S_bulk, which includes both the classical area term and quantum corrections from bulk entropy. The QES generalizes the Ryu-Takayanagi surface to include quantum effects.

questions.question: According to the ER=EPR conjecture, what connects maximally entangled particles?
questions.explanation: The ER=EPR conjecture (Maldacena-Susskind) proposes that entangled particles (EPR pairs) are connected by Einstein-Rosen bridges (wormholes). This suggests entanglement IS spacetime connectivity, providing a geometric interpretation of quantum correlations.

questions.question: Which of the following are implications of the island formula resolution of the information paradox? (Select all that apply)

options.text: The interior of the black hole is encoded in the radiation
Information is lost when black holes evaporate
Entanglement entropy follows the Page curve

questions.explanation: The island formula implies: (a) unitarity is preserved as information is returned in Hawking radiation; (b) the island region of the interior is encoded in the radiation; (d) the fine-grained entropy follows the Page curve. Information is NOT lost (c is false) - this was the whole point of resolving the paradox.
